repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem: Intro is not fancy. Solution: Make it fancy. --- An alternative to #38345 based on the suggestion from [this comment](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38345#issuecomment-4083023392). No test fixing yet, as there might be discussions. Disclaimer: I have not much clue of the best practice when it comes to the C code base. This is a result of trial and error based on the code from #38345. Demos (for the imaginary 0.12.0 release):
Now
This PR, default color scheme
Exact colors depend on the color scheme. I.e. the logo is not necessarily cyan+green, but I'd say it is fine.
This PR, `retrobox` color scheme
This PR, `catppuccin` color scheme
This PR, `fakedonalds` color scheme, !!!open at your own risk!!!

comment_data.body: i would drop this, it is misleading since we aren't actually annotating it as a URL?

`utf_ptr2char`


```suggestion
        seen_diagonal = seen_diagonal || (clen == 3 && utf_ptr2char(p) == 0x2572);  // Found "╲" diagonal logo part.
```
Makes sense. I also didn't *quite* like how it looked in some terminal emulators together with separator lines.

comment_regular_data.body: I would remove "I" from my shortmess for this.

Forced pushed after rebasing on `master`: - Remove padding from logo lines, since it centers the logo without them just fine. But padding interferes with highlighting if `EndOfBuffer` is different (see screenshots later). - Simplify check for the separator to be `bool is_sep = utfc_ptr2len(mesg) == 3 && utf_ptr2char(mesg) == 0x2500;` (following how it checks logo diagonal). - Adjust tests. If tests pass, I think this should be good to go. --- Just for posterity, here is how intro interacts with `EndOfBuffer` highlighting before and after this PR:
Before
After

The full project name doesn't appear to be spelled out anywhere on this new intro, other than in the URL for the chat.

I feel the text 'Neovim' should be present somewhere.

> The full project name doesn't appear to be spelled out anywhere on this new intro

It wasn't in the old intro either. I think it's fine.

Something to note: The thickness of the lines that make up neovim's logo depends on the terminal font. For example, the default kitty font displays the middle lines as thinner than the vertical ones: I still think it looks great and no changes are needed, but I thought I may as well point it out. --- Somewhat related, the new intro looks great in the Linux terminal, although due to the nature of the terminal I cannot take a screenshot to show it. If you are on Linux, just switch ttys and try it out, it looks very nice.

> The thickness of the lines that make up neovim's logo depends on the terminal font. For example, the default kitty font displays the middle lines as thinner than the vertical ones:

We should report this issue to kitty. The two symbols used are "Box Drawings Light Vertical" and "Box Drawings Light Diagonal Upper Left to Lower Right", which are the same series of symbols and are also "light" variants; "heavy" variants exist meanwhile. Therefore, inconsistencies should be considered bugs.

IIRC, modern terminals use custom logic to draw the "Box Drawings" series of symbols. When this custom logic exists, they draw these symbols directly and don't actually use what the font provides, which is why these terminals now display box symbols without gaps. The diagonals may not be supported, so they will be thinner, and gaps will exist.
maybe it's a bit late, but I think is missing the slanted cuts in the ends of each segment, which I think are distinctive part of the Neovim logo, as a trademark.
It looks perfect. Not interested in other opinions.
